instructions.1: Slice 1 lb flank steak thinly against the grain. Toss with 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tbsp cornstarch. Set aside 10 min.
instructions.2: Mix remaining soy sauce (3 tbsp), 2 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tbsp brown sugar, and 2 tbsp beef broth in a small bowl.
instructions.3: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
instructions.4: Add beef in a single layer. Sear 1-2 min until browned. Flip and cook 30 sec more. Remove to a plate.
instructions.5: Add 3 cups broccoli florets and 2 tbsp water to the wok. Cover and steam 2 min until bright green. Uncover, add 4 minced garlic cloves and 1 tsp ginger, stir-fry 30 sec.
instructions.6: Return beef to wok. Pour in sauce. Toss and cook 1-2 min until sauce thickens.
instructions.7: Serve immediately over steamed white rice.

instructions.1: Soak 8 oz rice noodles in warm water for 30 min until pliable. Drain.
instructions.2: Make the sauce: mix 3 tbsp fish sauce, 2 tbsp tamarind paste, 2 tbsp brown sugar, and 1 tbsp rice vinegar in a small bowl.
instructions.3: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a wok over high heat.
instructions.4: Add 6 oz medium shrimp (peeled) or chicken. Cook 2-3 min until done. Remove.
instructions.5: Add 2 beaten eggs to the wok, scramble until just set.
instructions.6: Add drained noodles and sauce. Toss 2-3 min until noodles absorb sauce.
instructions.7: Return protein to wok. Add 1 cup bean sprouts and 3 sliced green onions. Toss 1 min.
instructions.8: Serve with crushed peanuts, lime wedges, and extra fish sauce on the side.

instructions.1: Slice 1 lb chicken breast into bite-sized strips. Toss with 1 tbsp cornstarch, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and 1/2 tsp sesame oil.
instructions.2: Mix 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tbsp rice wine, 1 tsp sugar, and 1/2 cup chicken broth for the sauce.
instructions.3: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
instructions.4: Add chicken in a single layer. Sear 2 min until golden. Flip and cook 1 min more. Remove.
instructions.5: Add 1 cup broccoli florets, 1 sliced red bell pepper, and 1/2 cup snap peas. Stir-fry 2 min.
instructions.6: Return chicken. Pour in sauce. Toss 1-2 min until sauce thickens and coats everything.
instructions.7: Serve over steamed white rice or lo mein noodles.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 400°F.
instructions.2: Place 2 salmon fillets (6 oz each) on a lined baking sheet. Pat dry.
instructions.3: Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice. Season with salt, pepper, and 1/2 tsp garlic powder.
instructions.4: Arrange 1 bunch asparagus around the salmon. Drizzle asparagus with 1 tbsp olive oil and season.
instructions.5: Bake 12-15 minutes until salmon flakes easily and asparagus is tender.
instructions.6: Garnish with fresh dill and lemon wedges. Serve immediately.

instructions.1: Cut 1.5 lbs chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces. Pat dry.
instructions.2: Make the batter: whisk 3/4 cup cornstarch, 1/4 cup flour, 1 egg, 1/2 cup water, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
instructions.3: Dip chicken pieces in the batter. Fry in 350°F oil for 5-6 min until golden. Remove to wire rack.
instructions.4: Make sauce: combine 3 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 2 tbsp ketchup, 3 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 tsp ginger in a bowl.
instructions.5: Heat sauce in a saucepan until bubbling. thicken with 1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water.
instructions.6: Toss fried chicken in the sauce until well coated.
instructions.7: Serve over steamed rice. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.

instructions.1: Toss 1 lb large shrimp (peeled and deveined) with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/4 tsp paprika, salt and pepper.
instructions.2: Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
instructions.3: Cook shrimp 2-3 min per side until pink and curled.
instructions.4: Warm 6 small corn tortillas in a dry pan or directly over a gas flame.
instructions.5: Make slaw: toss 1 cup shredded purple cabbage, 2 tbsp mayo, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1/2 tsp cumin, and a pinch of salt.
instructions.6: Assemble tacos: shrimp, cabbage slaw, pico de gallo, and sliced avocado.
instructions.7: Finish with a squeeze of lime and hot sauce.

instructions.1: Brown 1 lb ground beef in a large pot over medium-high heat, breaking into crumbles. Season with salt and pepper. Cook 5-7 min until browned. Remove beef, leave fat.
instructions.2: Add 1 diced onion to the pot. Sauté 3 min until softened.
instructions.3: Add 3 minced garlic cloves and 1 tsp Italian seasoning. Cook 1 min.
instructions.4: Return beef. Add 1 can crushed tomatoes, 1/2 cup beef broth, 2 tbsp tomato paste.
instructions.5: Simmer on low for 25-30 min, stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens.
instructions.6: Meanwhile, cook 12 oz spaghetti according to package directions. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water before draining.
instructions.7: Toss drained pasta with sauce. Add pasta water as needed to loosen.
instructions.8: Serve topped with fresh parmesan and basil.

instructions.1: Season 2 chicken breasts (6 oz each) with salt, pepper, and 1 tsp garlic powder.
instructions.2: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook chicken 5-6 min per side until internal temp 165°F. Rest 5 min, then slice.
instructions.3: Meanwhile, cook 12 oz fettuccine according to package directions. Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain.
instructions.4: In the same skillet, melt 3 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add 2 cloves minced garlic and cook 30 sec.
instructions.5: Add 1 cup heavy cream and 1/2 cup parmesan. Stir until melted and smooth.
instructions.6: Toss fettuccine with sauce, adding pasta water as needed.
instructions.7: Top with sliced chicken, extra parmesan, and fresh parsley.

instructions.1: Brown 1 lb ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat. Season with salt and pepper.
instructions.2: Add 2 tbsp taco seasoning (or mix: 1 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/4 tsp paprika, pinch of garlic powder) and 1/4 cup water.
instructions.3: Simmer 3-4 min until liquid evaporates and beef is well coated.
instructions.4: Warm 8 taco shells in the oven at 350°F for 2-3 min.
instructions.5: Fill each shell with seasoned beef.
instructions.6: Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar, sour cream, and salsa.
instructions.7: Serve with lime wedges and jalapeños.

instructions.1: Pat 2 salmon fillets (6 oz each) dry. Brush with 1 tbsp olive oil.
instructions.2: Season generously with salt, pepper, and 1/2 tsp smoked paprika.
instructions.3: Heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
instructions.4: Place salmon skin-side up on the grill. Cook 4-5 min until golden with char marks.
instructions.5: Flip carefully. Cook another 3-4 min until internal temp reaches 145°F.
instructions.6: Serve with lemon wedges and fresh dill. Pairs well with roasted potatoes or a side salad.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 400°F. Pound 2 chicken breasts to even 1/2-inch thickness.
instructions.2: Set up breading station: flour in one dish, 2 beaten eggs in another, panko breadcrumbs mixed with 1/4 cup parmesan in a third.
instructions.3: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour, dip in egg, coat in panko mixture.
instructions.4: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet. Sear chicken 3 min per side until golden.
instructions.5: Top each breast with 1/4 cup marinara sauce and 1/4 cup mozzarella.
instructions.6: Bake 12-15 min until chicken is cooked through (165°F) and cheese is bubbly.
instructions.7: Serve over spaghetti with extra marinara and fresh basil.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 400°F.
instructions.2: Cut 2 sweet potatoes into 1/2-inch rounds. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet.
instructions.3: Roast sweet potatoes for 15 min, then set aside.
instructions.4: Season 2 bone-in pork chops (8 oz each) with salt, pepper, 1 tsp paprika, and 1/2 tsp garlic powder.
instructions.5: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high. Sear pork chops 4 min per side.
instructions.6: Transfer to oven-safe pan with sweet potatoes. Bake 10-12 min until internal temp reaches 145°F.
instructions.7: Rest 5 min before serving. Garnish with fresh thyme.

instructions.1: Cook 8 oz lo mein noodles or spaghetti according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
instructions.2: Mix sauce: 3 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp sesame oil, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp brown sugar, and 1/2 tsp chili flakes.
instructions.3: Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a wok over high heat.
instructions.4: Add 1 cup sliced mushrooms, 1 sliced red bell pepper, 1 cup shredded cabbage, and 1/2 cup shredded carrots.
instructions.5: Stir-fry 3-4 min until vegetables are crisp-tender.
instructions.6: Add noodles and sauce. Toss 2-3 min until well combined and noodles are heated through.
instructions.7: Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions.

instructions.1: Rub 3 lb pork shoulder with: 1 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp black pepper.
instructions.2: Place in a slow cooker with 1/2 cup apple cider vinegar and 1/2 cup water.
instructions.3: Cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4-5 hours until fork-tender.
instructions.4: Remove pork and shred with two forks, discarding fat.
instructions.5: Skim fat from cooking liquid. Mix 1 cup bbq sauce with 1/2 cup cooking liquid.
instructions.6: Toss shredded pork with the sauce mixture.
instructions.7: Serve on brioche buns topped with coleslaw.

instructions.1: Cook 12 oz linguine according to package directions. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water. Drain.
instructions.2: Heat 3 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
instructions.3: Add 1 lb large shrimp (peeled and deveined). Season with salt and pepper.
instructions.4: Cook shrimp 2 min per side until pink. Remove.
instructions.5: Add 6 minced garlic cloves to the skillet. Cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
instructions.6: Add 1/2 cup white wine or chicken broth, 2 tbsp lemon juice, and 1/4 cup pasta water. Simmer 2 min.
instructions.7: Return shrimp. Add cooked linguine and toss everything together.
instructions.8: Garnish with 1/4 cup parsley and extra lemon.

instructions.1: Season 1 lb ground beef with 2 tbsp taco seasoning, salt, and pepper.
instructions.2: Brown the beef in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add 1/4 cup water and simmer 2 min.
instructions.3: Make cilantro-lime rice: combine 1 cup cooked white rice, 1 tbsp lime juice, and 1/4 cup chopped cilantro.
instructions.4: Build bowls: rice base, topped with seasoned beef, 1/2 cup black beans, 1/2 cup corn, 1/4 cup pico de gallo, 1/4 cup shredded cheese.
instructions.5: Add 1/4 sliced avocado and a dollop of sour cream.
instructions.6: Drizzle with 1 tbsp chipotle mayo or regular mayo mixed with lime juice.
instructions.7: Top with tortilla strips and extra cilantro.

instructions.1: Heat 4 cups vegetable broth in a saucepan and keep warm over low heat.
instructions.2: Sauté 8 oz mixed mushrooms (cremini, shiitake) in 2 tbsp butter until golden, about 5 min. Season with salt. Set aside.
instructions.3: In the same pan, melt 2 tbsp butter over medium. Add 1 shallot (minced) and 1 cup arborio rice. Toast 2 min, stirring.
instructions.4: Add 1/2 cup white wine and stir until absorbed.
instructions.5: Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring frequently, waiting until each addition is absorbed before adding more.
instructions.6: Continue for 18-20 min until rice is creamy and al dente.
instructions.7: Stir in sautéed mushrooms, 1/2 cup parmesan, and 1 tbsp butter. Season with salt and pepper.
instructions.8: Serve immediately with extra parmesan and fresh thyme.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 400°F.
instructions.2: Pat dry 2 chicken breasts. Season with salt, pepper, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp dried thyme, and 1/2 tsp garlic powder.
instructions.3: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken 3 min per side until golden.
instructions.4: Slice 1 lemon and place lemon slices under and around the chicken in the pan.
instructions.5: Add 1/2 cup chicken broth and 2 tbsp butter to the pan.
instructions.6: Roast in oven 12-15 min until internal temp reaches 165°F.
instructions.7: Rest 5 min. Slice and serve drizzled with pan juices and fresh herbs.

instructions.1: Heat 1 tbsp coconut oil in a wok over medium heat.
instructions.2: Add 2-3 tbsp green curry paste and fry 1-2 min until fragrant.
instructions.3: Add 1 lb chicken breast (sliced) and coat in the paste. Cook 3 min.
instructions.4: Pour in 1 can coconut milk and 1/2 cup chicken broth.
instructions.5: Add 1 cup Thai basil leaves, 1 sliced red bell pepper, and 1/2 cup bamboo shoots.
instructions.6: Simmer 12-15 min until chicken is cooked through.
instructions.7: Season with 1 tbsp fish sauce and 1 tbsp palm sugar or brown sugar.
instructions.8: Serve over jasmine rice with Thai basil and sliced chilies.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 375°F.
instructions.2: Cut the tops off 4 bell peppers and remove seeds. Brush with olive oil and season inside.
instructions.3: Brown 1 lb ground turkey or beef in a skillet. Season with salt, pepper, and 1 tsp cumin.
instructions.4: Add 1 can diced tomatoes, 1/2 cup cooked rice, and 1/2 cup black beans. Mix well.
instructions.5: Stuff the mixture into each bell pepper. Place in a baking dish.
instructions.6: Add 1/2 cup water or broth to the dish. Cover with foil.
instructions.7: Bake 35 min. Remove foil, top with shredded cheese, bake 10 more min until cheese is bubbly.

instructions.1: Cut 1.5 lbs chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces. Pat dry.
instructions.2: Make batter: whisk 3/4 cup cornstarch, 1/4 cup flour, 1 egg, 1/2 cup water. Dip chicken.
instructions.3: Deep fry at 350°F for 5-6 min until golden. Drain on wire rack.
instructions.4: Make orange sauce: combine 3 tbsp orange juice, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 3 tbsp sugar, 1 tbsp orange zest in a saucepan. Bring to simmer.
instructions.5: Thicken with 1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water.
instructions.6: Toss fried chicken in the sauce until well coated.
instructions.7: Serve over steamed rice. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.

instructions.1: Preheat broiler to high.
instructions.2: Mix 2 tbsp white miso paste, 2 tbsp mirin, 2 tbsp sake, 1 tbsp sugar, and 1 tbsp soy sauce in a bowl until smooth.
instructions.3: Pat 4 cod fillets (5 oz each) dry. Place on a lined baking sheet.
instructions.4: Brush the miso glaze generously over the top and sides of each fillet.
instructions.5: Broil 4-5 minutes until glaze is caramelized and fish flakes easily.
instructions.6: Serve over steamed rice with edamame on the side.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 400°F. Slice 2 eggplants into 1/2-inch rounds. Salt both sides and let sit 20 min to draw out moisture. Pat dry.
instructions.2: Set up breading station: flour, beaten eggs, and panko breadcrumbs mixed with 1/4 cup parmesan.
instructions.3: Dip eggplant slices in flour, egg, then breadcrumb mixture. Place on baking sheets.
instructions.4: Bake 20 min until golden and tender, flipping halfway.
instructions.5: In a baking dish, layer: marinara sauce, eggplant slices, mozzarella, parmesan. Repeat layers.
instructions.6: Top with remaining marinara and mozzarella. Bake 20 min until bubbly.
instructions.7: Let rest 5 min. Serve over spaghetti with fresh basil.

instructions.1: Slice 1 lb beef ribeye or sirloin very thinly against the grain.
instructions.2: Marinate in 3 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp sesame oil, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 4 cloves minced garlic, 1 tsp ginger, and 2 tbsp water for 15 min.
instructions.3: Heat a grill pan or cast iron over high heat.
instructions.4: Cook beef in a single layer for 1-2 min per side until caramelized (work in batches).
instructions.5: Serve over steamed rice with kimchi.
instructions.6: Garnish with sesame seeds, sliced green onions, and a drizzle of gochujang.

instructions.1: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium heat.
instructions.2: Sauté 1 diced onion and 1 diced red bell pepper for 5 min. Add 3 cloves minced garlic, cook 1 min.
instructions.3: Add 1.5 cups Arborio rice and toast for 2 min, stirring.
instructions.4: Add 3 cups warm chicken broth, 1/2 tsp saffron threads, 1 tsp smoked paprika, and 1/2 cup white wine. Bring to simmer.
instructions.5: Arrange 8 oz shrimp, 8 oz mussels, and 6 oz calamari on top. DO NOT STIR after this point.
instructions.6: Simmer 20-25 min until rice is cooked and seafood is open and cooked through.
instructions.7: Let rest 5 min. Garnish with fresh parsley and lemon wedges.

instructions.1: Preheat oven to 400°F.
instructions.2: Cut 2 sweet potatoes into wedges. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper. Roast 15 min.
instructions.3: Season 2 bone-in pork chops (8 oz each) with salt, pepper, 1 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp garlic powder.
instructions.4: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high. Sear pork chops 4 min per side.
instructions.5: Transfer to oven with sweet potatoes. Bake 10-12 min until internal temp 145°F.
instructions.6: Rest 5 min. Garnish with fresh thyme.

instructions.1: Rub 3 lb pork shoulder with 1 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper.
instructions.2: Slow cook with 1/2 cup apple cider vinegar and 1/2 cup water on low 8 hours.
instructions.3: Shred with forks, discarding fat.
instructions.4: Mix 1 cup BBQ sauce with 1/2 cup cooking liquid. Toss with pork.
instructions.5: Serve on brioche buns with coleslaw.

instructions.1: Cook 8 oz lo mein noodles. Drain and set aside.
instructions.2: Mix 3 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp sesame oil, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp brown sugar.
instructions.3: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok over high. Stir-fry 2 cups mixed vegetables 3-4 min.
instructions.4: Add noodles and sauce. Toss 2-3 min.
instructions.5: Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
